authors: Alexander Golbraikh, Eugene Muratov, Denis Fourches, Alexander Tropsha
title: Data Set Modelability by QSAR
journal_or_venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
notes: Verified via Crossref. Title in the reference includes "(MODI)" as a parenthetical; the actual published title is simply "Data Set Modelability by QSAR" — MODI is the modelability index defined in the paper, not part of the title. All other details (authors, journal, 2014, vol 54, issue 1, pp. 1-4, DOI) match exactly.

authors: Irene Luque Ruiz, Miguel Ángel Gómez-Nieto
title: Regression Modelability Index: A New Index for Prediction of the Modelability of Data Sets in the Development of QSAR Regression Models
journal_or_venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
notes: Confirmed via PubMed (PMID 30205684) and ACS JCIM publisher page. This is the paper introducing the Regression Modelability Index (RMODI) for continuous/regression QSAR endpoints. Full record: J. Chem. Inf. Model. 2018, 58(10), 2069-2084. Authors: Irene Luque Ruiz and Miguel Angel Gomez-Nieto (Univ. of Cordoba, Spain). Note: RMODI extends the earlier classification-oriented MODI (Golbraikh et al., "Data set modelability by QSAR," J. Chem. Inf. Model. 2014, 54(1), 1-4) to continuous response variables; a related book chapter "Dataset Modelability by QSAR: Continuous Response Variable" (Springer, 2021, doi:10.1007/978-3-030-83244-5_7) also covers this topic if that is the intended reference. If the citation specifically refers to RMODI, the confirmed paper above is correct.

authors: Barbara Zdrazil, Eloy Felix, Fiona Hunter, Emma J Manners, James Blackshaw, Sybilla Corbett, Marleen de Veij, Harris Ioannidis, David Mendez Lopez, Juan F Mosquera, Maria Paula Magarinos, Nicolas Bosc, Ricardo Arcila, Tevfik Kizilören, Anna Gaulton, A Patrícia Bento, Melissa F Adasme, Peter Monecke, Gregory A Landrum, Andrew R Leach
title: The ChEMBL Database in 2023: a drug discovery platform spanning multiple bioactivity data types and time periods
notes: Confirmed via Oxford Academic (NAR) and PMC (PMC10767899). Published online 2 Nov 2023; issue dated 5 January 2024. Note: the citing reference labels this as covering "release 33/37" and dates it 2023 — the article self-identifies as "in 2023" but the formal publication year of the print volume is 2024. The DOI gkad1004 is authoritative. This is the ChEMBL release 33 paper. There is no separate "ChEMBL Database in 2024" NAR paper by Zdrazil; a 2025 J. Cheminformatics paper "Fifteen years of ChEMBL" exists but is a different venue/article. NOTE: this 2024 platform article describes ChEMBL release 33; the dataset actually used here is ChEMBL release 37 (separate citation, ref 10).

title: ChEMBL Database, Release 37
